P3HR-001.title: SHRM 2025 Talent Trends — The Role of AI in HR Continues to Expand
P3HR-001.author: SHRM Research (n=2,040 US HR pros, Feb 2025)
P3HR-001.excerpt: 43% of organizations now use AI in HR tasks, up from 26% in 2024. 51% leverage AI for recruiting (66% job descriptions, 44% resume screening, 32% automating candidate searches). 89% of HR pros using AI for recruiting say it saves time/efficiency; 36% report cost reduction; 24% improved top-candidate identification. 67% disagreed their organization has been proactive in training employees to work with AI.
P3HR-001.relevance: Canonical 2025 US HR-side AI-adoption baseline. Establishes that HR's AI usage is real and majority for recruiting but still emerging; HR pros feel un-trained.
P3HR-001.credibility: high — primary survey of representative HR pop, widely cited

P3HR-002.title: The 'AI race' has fostered better hiring decisions — and mistrust, survey finds
P3HR-002.author: HR Dive / Checkr 2025 survey
P3HR-002.excerpt: 70% US hiring managers say AI makes hiring faster and better with fewer recruiter resources, BUT 68% report being more involved in hiring than last year. Only 21% of recruiters are very confident their systems aren't rejecting qualified candidates, despite over half having delegated most screening to AI. 91% have caught or suspected AI-driven candidate fakery: 32% fake voices/backgrounds, 32% AI scripts, 18% deepfakes. 39% now conduct more in-person interviews. Greenhouse CEO: 'Candidates are doing whatever they can to break through the noise, while talent acquisition teams are drowning in so many applications.'
P3HR-002.relevance: Central trust-gap evidence. Demonstrates the two-sided collapse: hiring mgrs love AI for speed yet recruiters distrust their own pipelines, and 91% have hit fakery. Direct support for 'verifiable identity' thesis.
P3HR-002.credibility: high — Checkr/Greenhouse survey, well-sourced trade press

P3HR-003.title: AI Adoption in Recruiting: 2025 Year in Review
P3HR-003.author: Herohunt (vendor)
P3HR-003.excerpt: 51% of firms already deployed AI in hiring, expected to reach 68% by EOY 2025; 76% US vs 36% EU. 82% of AI-using companies use it to review resumes; 41% piloted AI scheduling; sourcing time -50%; 23% use AI to conduct/evaluate interviews. 62% employers expect AI in most or all hiring stages by 2026. Tool landscape: LinkedIn Hiring Assistant, HireVue (~20M video interviews Q1 2024 alone), Eightfold AI, Beamery, Phenom, Paradox Olivia, HireEZ, SeekOut. Claim: AI 'scales up a recruiter's capacity'; companies filled 64% more jobs / 33% more candidates per recruiter without added headcount.
P3HR-003.relevance: Tool-landscape map + task-automation %s. US-EU split useful for our overseas-market framing.
P3HR-003.credibility: medium — vendor blog, aggregates third-party stats reasonably but with optimistic spin on displacement

P3HR-004.title: Josh Bersin's top AI headline for 2025: It's all about the agents
P3HR-004.author: HR Executive interviewing Josh Bersin
P3HR-004.excerpt: Agentic AI = 'huge transition.' Bersin: recruiters will evolve from operational 'order takers' to strategic partners. 70% of TA leaders currently neglect reskilling. Vendors mentioned: Workday HiredScore, Oracle, Microsoft Copilot, Eightfold, Galileo. AI is 'work enhancing' not 'job replacement.' 2025 is year of the 'Superworker' — employee AI-empowered to deliver orders-of-magnitude greater productivity.
P3HR-004.relevance: Authoritative HR-industry-analyst voice; sets the optimistic framing that's the dominant story HR tells itself. Useful as benchmark belief.
P3HR-004.credibility: high — Bersin is the most-cited HR analyst, but has industry conflict-of-interest

P3HR-005.title: Tech Companies Are Blaming Layoffs on AI — What's Really Going On
P3HR-005.author: Recruiting News Network
P3HR-005.excerpt: Tech firms (Atlassian, Block, Amazon) attribute layoffs to AI. Goldman Sachs estimate: full AI deployment would risk ~2.5% of US employment. Sectors with genuine strain: marketing consulting, graphic design, office admin, call centers. Younger tech workers in AI-exposed roles saw unemployment +3% early 2025.
P3HR-005.relevance: Counter-balance to Bersin optimism. Confirms that 'AI did it' is partly a narrative cover for normal cost-cutting, but real entry-level squeeze exists.

P3HR-006.title: 1 in 4 Candidate Profiles Will Be Fake by 2028. Is Your Verification Process Ready?
P3HR-006.author: Staffing Hub citing Gartner July 2025
P3HR-006.excerpt: Gartner July 2025: by 2028, 1 in 4 candidate profiles globally will be partially or fully fake. 41% of staffing buyers already experiencing candidate fraud. 90% HR workers reported increased AI-generated applications in 2025. Only 19% hiring mgrs 'extremely confident' their verification process catches fraudulent applicants. Cybersecurity firm: 23.2% of applicants flagged as fraud risks in 3-month window. 23% of businesses lost >$50K from fraudulent placement; 10% >$100K. FTC: job-scam losses $90M→$501M (2020-2024).
P3HR-006.relevance: Strongest macro tailwind for our 'verified professional identity' thesis. Buyer-side willingness-to-pay for identity assurance is rising fast.
P3HR-006.credibility: high — Gartner primary, FTC/FBI data, multiple corroborating angles

P3HR-007.title: 2025 AI in Hiring Survey Report
P3HR-007.author: Insight Global (n=1,005 US hiring managers, Oct 2024)
P3HR-007.excerpt: 99% hiring mgrs use AI in hiring; 98% saw efficiency gains; 95% anticipate increased AI investment. Candidate side: 40% use AI for application materials, 31% for interview prep, 21% to research companies, 8% for salary expectations. 88% hiring mgrs claim they can detect candidate AI use. 54% care whether candidates use AI. 93% emphasize importance of human involvement.
P3HR-007.relevance: Validates near-ubiquitous AI use among hiring mgrs and significant minority candidate-side usage. Shows mutual surveillance dynamic.
P3HR-007.credibility: medium-high — staffing firm primary survey, sample skews mid-market US

P3HR-008.title: HR.com Future of Recruitment Technologies 2025-26
P3HR-008.author: HR.com (via Eightfold redistribution)
P3HR-008.excerpt: 14% extensive AI use, 49% some use, 37% none (was 73% none in 2023). 27% of leader orgs use AI extensively vs 3% of laggards — bimodal. Use cases: interview Q generation 67%, JD writing 65%, resume filter 44%, candidate comms 43%. Top concerns: bias 58%, depersonalization 51%, legal 50%. Only 43% rate their TA stack good/excellent; only 23% would rehire 76-100% of recent hires.
P3HR-008.relevance: Adds a leader-vs-laggard bimodality view + TA stack dissatisfaction signal (low rehire confidence = signal-quality problem we could solve).
P3HR-008.credibility: high — established HR.com research, distributed via Eightfold (vendor caveat noted)

P3HR-009.title: HR's Perception Issue (I Hate It Here newsletter)
P3HR-009.author: Hebba Youssef
P3HR-009.excerpt: 'Ever tell someone you work in HR and their reaction and energy towards you just… changes?' HR pros struggle against the perception of being 'paper pushers.' Burnout driver: 'A lot of HR teams spend more time fixing problems than focusing on proactive work.' Reframing prescribed: demonstrate 'impact on things like growth, revenue and innovation' and speak the 'language of the C-suite.' Newsletter has 136K HR/People Ops subscribers.
P3HR-009.relevance: Confirms strong identity/status-anxiety motivation. HR pros want a stage on which to PROVE their business impact. This is the C-side latent demand we could tap.
P3HR-009.credibility: high — first-person voice from largest HR practitioner-community newsletter, 136K verified subs

P3HR-010.title: LinkedIn Recruiter — AI-Assisted Search and Projects / Hiring Assistant
P3HR-010.author: LinkedIn Talent Solutions
P3HR-010.excerpt: LinkedIn Hiring Assistant GA in English end of September 2025. Charter customers: reviewed 62% fewer profiles, saved 4+ hours per role, 69% higher InMail acceptance. AI-Assisted Search uses GenAI on LinkedIn's 1B+ professionals, 65M companies, 40K skills. LinkedIn Future of Recruiting 2025: GenAI saves recruiting teams ~20% of their work week.
P3HR-010.relevance: LinkedIn embedding GenAI into the exact workflow recruiters live in. Cements LinkedIn as default. Major lock-in headwind for any new-platform play.
P3HR-010.credibility: high — vendor announcement of factual product launch, customer metrics self-reported

P3HR-011.title: HireEZ vs SeekOut: AI Sourcing Platforms Compared (2026)
P3HR-011.author: Pin (recruiting tools blog)
P3HR-011.excerpt: HireEZ: 800M+ profile index from 45+ public sources, March 2025 agentic-AI launch, $169/user/month. SeekOut: 1B+ profiles, contextual AI, $799/seat/month, $10-90K annual contracts. SeekOut Spot pairs agentic AI with human recruiters. Eightfold: less sourcing, more talent intelligence platform — internal mobility + DEI. LinkedIn Corporate: $10.8-15K/seat/year.
P3HR-011.relevance: Concrete economics of B-side market we'd compete in. SeekOut/Eightfold/HireEZ are validation that a well-curated talent graph commands $10-90K ARR per buyer.
P3HR-011.credibility: medium — vendor-comparison blog, but pricing/scale info matches public sources

P3HR-012.title: Recruit Holdings / Indeed-Glassdoor 1,300 layoffs (cited)
P3HR-012.author: multiple sources via Recruiting News Network / Programs.com
P3HR-012.excerpt: Recruit Holdings (Indeed+Glassdoor parent) cut 1,300 jobs (~6% global workforce) in July 2025; CEO Hisayuki 'Deko' Idekoba said 'AI is changing the world.' ~55K layoffs in 2025 directly attributed to AI by Challenger Gray. >100K employees impacted by AI-driven layoffs in 2025 (45+ CEOs). Entry-level and junior roles especially exposed.
P3HR-012.relevance: Counter to Bersin optimism. Recruiter/sourcer rung is materially under threat. Junior recruiters and sourcers are the most-AI-exposed in HR.
P3HR-012.credibility: medium-high — corroborated by multiple trade sources, CEO statement direct

P3HR-013.title: AI Fluency: Core HR Competency To Develop (AIHR)
P3HR-013.author: AIHR (Academy to Innovate HR)
P3HR-013.excerpt: AI fluency = ability to collaborate with AI confidently, augmenting human judgment. AIHR Artificial Intelligence for HR Certificate: $975 individual / $1,850 yr full access, 35 hrs, SHRM/HRCI CE credits. Zapier requires 100% of new hires demonstrate AI fluency, tiered by role. HR pros with AI skills command 20-35% salary premium. 89% senior HR leaders (CNBC Workforce Exec Council 2025) expect AI to significantly impact jobs in 2026.
P3HR-013.relevance: Concrete definition of 'AI fluency' as HR-specific competency + credentialing economics. Important: AI cred is 'supplementary, not foundational' — SHRM-CP/PHR still gate.
P3HR-013.credibility: medium-high — AIHR has vendor interest in credentialing, but stats consistent with broader market data

P3HR-014.title: PeopleGPT — Q Hamirani / Human Cloud HR Community
P3HR-014.author: Q Hamirani / Human Cloud (acquired Mar 2025)
P3HR-014.excerpt: PeopleGPT founded by Q Hamirani (Chief People Officer, HighLevel; ex-Airbnb Live & Work Anywhere lead). 600+ global members across 6 continents and 200+ cities — AI-focused People Leaders. Acquired by Human Cloud March 17, 2025; became Human Cloud HR Community. Hamirani is now board member.
P3HR-014.relevance: Concrete pocket of forward-leaning HR/People leaders who self-organize OFF LinkedIn (Slack-style community). This is exactly the seed-cohort for a new product to recruit. Small (600), but high-status / high-influence.
P3HR-014.credibility: high — first-party announcement, acquisition is public news

P3HR-015.title: I Hate It Here — Hebba Youssef community/newsletter
P3HR-015.author: Hebba Youssef (founder), Workweek
P3HR-015.excerpt: 136,000+ HR/People Ops subscribers. Newsletter, podcast and community. AI Readiness Gap 4-part mini-series with TriNet. Audience: 'jaded, overworked and emotionally burned out HR/People Operations professionals.' Twice-weekly cadence.
P3HR-015.relevance: Sizing reference for engaged HR community OFF LinkedIn (but still ON LinkedIn-adjacent Substack/podcast). 136K is real but small relative to ~3M+ US HR pros — signal of niche-engaged minority.
P3HR-015.credibility: high — public subscriber count, prominent in HR community

P3HR-016.title: SHRM — Automation, Generative AI, and Job Displacement Risk in HR Employment
P3HR-016.author: SHRM Research
P3HR-016.excerpt: SHRM analysis explicitly maps which HR sub-roles are most exposed to GenAI: data entry, basic resume screening, scheduling, simple FAQ answering, junior sourcing. Strategic, advisory, leadership and complex case-management roles much less exposed. Conclusion: HR transformation, not HR elimination — but the 'bottom of the ladder' is being kicked.
P3HR-016.relevance: Validates the asymmetry: SOURCERS and junior recruiters under real threat; CHROs/People-Ops strategists safe and rising. Bimodal labor market within HR.
P3HR-016.credibility: high — SHRM is the authoritative HR body

P3HR-017.title: Fisher Phillips — AI Hiring Bias Laws in EU/UK + NYC Local Law 144
P3HR-017.author: Fisher Phillips LLP
P3HR-017.excerpt: NYC Local Law 144 requires annual bias audits of automated employment decision tools + candidate notice; penalties $500-$1,500 per violation. EU AI Act classifies recruiting/screening/promotion/perf-eval AI as 'high-risk' with strict transparency/monitoring/training obligations; bans on harmful/discriminatory AI took effect Feb 2 2025. Banned uses: deceptive manipulation, social scoring, biometric trait inference, emotion recognition in interviews/assessments. Active state laws or pending: CO, UT, IL, NJ, MA, ME, PA, CT, NY, NM, TX, VA.
P3HR-017.relevance: Regulatory headwind that increases demand for transparent, candidate-controlled professional data (potential angle for our product). Also raises B-side compliance friction.
P3HR-017.credibility: high — law firm primary commentary, well-sourced

P3HR-018.title: AI job requirements for 2026: key roles requiring AI skills
P3HR-018.author: Amply / aggregated job posting data
P3HR-018.excerpt: Job postings including AI skills offer 28% higher salaries (~$18,000 more). 120,000+ jobs monthly in 2025 request AI skills. Senior leadership roles — Chief People Analytics Officer, AI Workforce Strategy VP — commanding $200-275K+. HR-specific AI specialist roles $85-130K (entry/mid), $100-165K (mid), $170-200K+ (senior).
P3HR-018.relevance: Hard economics of the AI-fluent HR premium. Establishes that real money is on the table for HR pros who can credibly demonstrate AI capability — i.e., latent demand for a place to demonstrate it.
P3HR-018.credibility: medium — aggregator, but consistent with corroborating Lightcast/Indeed reports

P3HR-019.title: Deepfake interviews and fake candidates: How recruiters can detect them
P3HR-019.author: Metaview
P3HR-019.excerpt: Common signs of fake/AI candidates: overly polished language, identical sentence structures, unrealistic promotions in short timeframes, vague responsibility descriptions, lack of concrete examples. Red flags: candidate unusually well-matched, mirroring JD language, confident high-level answers but struggle on 'go deeper, explain decisions, give specific examples.'
P3HR-019.relevance: Concrete detection heuristics recruiters use. Confirms work-products / 'show your work' artifacts are the natural antidote — which aligns with our 'capability evidence over experience' thesis.
P3HR-019.credibility: medium — vendor blog but methodology consistent with broader industry guidance

P3HR-020.title: Gartner — AI Revolution and Cost Pressures Drive Top TA Trends 2026
P3HR-020.author: Gartner
P3HR-020.excerpt: AI revolution + cost pressures = top 4 TA trends 2026. Includes: AI agents reshaping recruiter role, candidate fraud requiring identity verification, skills-based hiring acceleration, internal mobility prioritized over external hiring.
P3HR-020.relevance: Gartner's authoritative TA trend list 2026. Validates that 'verified identity' and 'skills evidence' have moved into the executive vocabulary of TA leaders.
P3HR-020.credibility: high — Gartner is the most-cited analyst firm in TA strategy

P3HR-021.title: Josh Bersin — The Rise of the Superworker
P3HR-021.author: Josh Bersin
P3HR-021.excerpt: 'Superworker' defined as an employee AI-empowered to deliver orders-of-magnitude greater productivity, creativity, or service. AI is 'work enhancing,' not 'job replacement.' Time-to-market/quality/productivity improvements 100x greater than cost savings of reducing routine work.
P3HR-021.relevance: Defines the optimistic narrative HR pros are using to position AI internally. This narrative is what HR pros will pay to *demonstrate they embody*.
P3HR-021.credibility: high — Bersin author, widely-syndicated thesis, but industry-aligned framing

P3HR-022.title: I Hate It Here Newsletter (Workweek)
P3HR-022.author: Hebba Youssef / Workweek
P3HR-022.excerpt: Newsletter + podcast + community. 136K subscribers. Twice-weekly cadence. Topics include HR Leader Profiles, AI Readiness Gap, HR's Perception Issue. Workweek (parent) hosts multiple creator-led B2B media properties.
P3HR-022.relevance: Confirms Substack+podcast as the off-LinkedIn channel for HR thought-leadership. Important pattern: HR thought leaders do not migrate platforms — they layer media on TOP of LinkedIn.

P3HR-023.title: AI Courses for HR Professionals — AIHR
P3HR-023.author: AIHR
P3HR-023.excerpt: 11 named AI courses for HR pros. Notes that 60-70% of HR leaders report their teams need AI upskilling, 'AI literacy' appears in increasing HR job descriptions, and AI-HR credentials are 'supplementary, not foundational' — SHRM-CP / PHR still gate.
P3HR-023.relevance: Concrete signal that HR pros are actively buying AI credentials. But conservative credential market means new HR-credentialing platform faces high incumbency.

P3HR-024.title: Microsoft — Fake Employees, Real Threat: Decentralized Identity to Combat Deepfake Hiring
P3HR-024.author: Microsoft Security
P3HR-024.excerpt: Amazon's CSO: since April 2024, blocked >1,800 suspected North Korean scammers from getting hired; volume +27% per quarter. Recommends shifting identity verification to assessment stage, not late background check. Mentions Microsoft Entra Verified ID for decentralized identity in hiring.
P3HR-024.relevance: Big-tech buyer voice confirming identity verification has become a procurement priority. Maps to our 'verifiable identity' B-side monetization path.
P3HR-024.credibility: high — Microsoft + Amazon CSO direct quote

P3HR-025.title: Top 9 Slack Communities for HR and People Ops Professionals
P3HR-025.author: Deel
P3HR-025.excerpt: Lattice 'Resources for Humans' Slack ~23,000 HR leaders. #People community ~6,000 people ops/HR/talent mgmt pros. Fractional People People = curated community for fractional CHROs. Multiple specialized HR slacks for AI, DEI, comp & benefits, etc.
P3HR-025.relevance: Sizes the OFF-LinkedIn engaged HR cohort: Slack 20-30K aggregate, Hebba 136K newsletter, PeopleGPT 600, Josh Bersin Academy similar magnitude. Total engaged tier is low six figures globally — a workable seed cohort but not a market on its own.
P3HR-025.credibility: medium — vendor compilation, but member counts publicly verifiable
